mission.title: Install and Configure Interlink on Kubernetes
mission.description: InterLink provides an abstraction layer for executing Kubernetes pods on remote resources, enabling the development of provider-specific plugins for the Kubernetes Virtual Kubelet interface. Installing InterLink allows you to leverage its capabilities for managing container execution lifecycles across various environments.

steps.title: Install interLink CLI
steps.description:
Download the interLink installer:
```bash
curl -Lo interlink https://github.com/interlink-hq/interLink/releases/latest/download/interlink-linux-amd64
chmod +x interlink
sudo mv interlink /usr/local/bin/
```
See https://interlink-hq.github.io/interLink/ for detailed docs.

steps.title: Deploy the Virtual Kubelet node
steps.description:
interLink creates virtual nodes in your cluster that offload pods to remote providers:
```bash
interlink install --provider slurm --namespace interlink-system
```
This deploys the Virtual Kubelet and interLink API server.

steps.title: Configure the provider sidecar
steps.description:
Each provider (SLURM, HTCondor, Docker, etc.) requires a sidecar configuration.

See the provider-specific docs:
```bash
# List available provider examples
ls -la /etc/interlink/

# For SLURM provider, configure the endpoint
cat > /etc/interlink/provider.yaml << 'EOF'
provider:
  type: slurm
  host: slurm-login.example.com
  port: 22
EOF
```
Full documentation: https://interlink-hq.github.io/interLink/

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check the virtual node is ready:
```bash
kubectl get nodes
kubectl get pods -n interlink-system
```
The virtual node should appear as Ready in the node list.

resolution.summary: interLink is installed with a Virtual Kubelet node for offloading workloads to remote compute providers.

uninstall.title: Remove interLink components
uninstall.description:
```bash
kubectl delete namespace interlink-system
sudo rm /usr/local/bin/interlink
```

upgrade.title: Download latest release
upgrade.description:
```bash
curl -Lo interlink https://github.com/interlink-hq/interLink/releases/latest/download/interlink-linux-amd64
chmod +x interlink
sudo mv interlink /usr/local/bin/
```

troubleshooting.title: Virtual node not appearing
troubleshooting.description:
Check the Virtual Kubelet logs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app=virtual-kubelet -n interlink-system
```

troubleshooting.title: Pods stuck in Pending on virtual node
troubleshooting.description:
Check the interLink API server logs for provider connectivity issues:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app=interlink -n interlink-system
```

troubleshooting.title: Provider authentication errors
troubleshooting.description: Verify OAuth2 or bearer token configuration in the interLink config.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have kubectl installed and configured to communicate with your Kubernetes cluster.
